questions.question: What is the result of adding two negative integers?
questions.explanation: When signs are the same, add the absolute values and keep the common sign.

questions.question: How do you add integers with different signs?
questions.correct_answer: Subtract the smaller absolute value from the larger and use the sign of the larger value
questions.explanation: For example, $(-7) + 5 = -2$ because 7 is larger than 5.

questions.question: What is the additive inverse of an integer?
questions.correct_answer: The same number with the opposite sign
questions.explanation: The sum of an integer and its additive inverse is always 0.

questions.question: What is the rule for multiplying two negative integers?
What happens when you multiply an integer by -1?
questions.correct_answer: The sign of the integer changes
questions.explanation: Multiplication by -1 gives the additive inverse of the number.

questions.question: Is division of integers commutative?
questions.explanation:
$a ÷ b$ is not the same as $b ÷ a$. For example, $(-8) ÷ 2 
eq 2 ÷ (-8)$.

questions.question: What is the Multiplicative Identity for integers?
questions.explanation: Any integer multiplied by 1 remains unchanged.

questions.question: State the Distributive Property of multiplication over addition.
questions.correct_answer: $a 	imes (b + c) = (a 	imes b) + (a 	imes c)$
questions.explanation: This property allows us to multiply a number by a sum by distributing the multiplication.

questions.question: What is the result of $a ÷ 0$?
questions.explanation: Division by zero is not mathematically defined for any number.

questions.question: How do integers help in representing profit and loss?
questions.correct_answer: Profit is represented by positive integers and loss by negative integers
questions.explanation: This provides a clear way to calculate net financial standing.

notes.title: Number Line Movement
notes.content: Add positive: move right. Add negative: move left. Subtract negative: move right.

notes.title: Product Sign Rules
notes.content: Even number of negative factors = Positive product; Odd number of negative factors = Negative product.

notes.title: Closure Property
notes.content: Integers are closed under addition, subtraction, and multiplication, but NOT always under division.

notes.title: BODMAS Priority
notes.content: Always perform division and multiplication before addition and subtraction unless brackets indicate otherwise.

facts.fact: The sum of any two integers is always an integer.
0 is neither a positive nor a negative integer.
The absolute value of an integer is its distance from zero, regardless of direction.
Sea level is usually represented as 0 in integer applications.
